item.title: UK urged to boost carbon capture projects
item.description: Trade bodies call on government to take action on investments for CCUS

item.title: Repairs at world’s largest gas-to-liquids plant to cost ‘well below $500 million,’ says Shell executive
item.description: Qatar's Pearl gas-to-liquids is the world’s largest gas-to-liquids facility, capable of handling up to 1.6 billion cubic feet per day of wellhead gas

item.title: Hope on hold as new site sought for Europe's second offshore hydrogen pilot
item.description: Chief executive Matthieu Guesne admits that 10 MW facility will be delayed until new location is found

item.title: Bluewater finds buyer for FPSO first used in 1997
item.description: FPSO is one of two units that Bluewater has been trying for years to redeploy

item.title: Middle East driller reports 90% plunge in net profit amid suspension of offshore rigs
item.description: Arabian Drilling is a leading onshore and offshore drilling contractor headquartered in Al Khobar, Saudi Arabia, which manages up to 60 drilling rigs

item.title: 'Hard rock': Tough going at Murphy's latest Africa exploration probe
item.description: Ivory Coast wildcat behind schedule after encountering challenging downhole conditions

item.title: 'Hard rock': Tough going at Murphy's latest Africa exploration well
item.description: Ivory Coast wellt behind schedule after encountering challenging downhole conditions

item.title: Aramco Q1 profit jumps 25% to $32.5 billion despite disruption at Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The company's East-West Pipeline sharply ramped up oil supplies to reach its maximum capacity of 7 million barrels per day in Q1

item.title: Aramco Q1 profit up 25% to $32.5 billion despite disruption at Hormuz
item.description: Company ramped up oil supply via East-West Pipeline to maximum capacity of 7 million barrels per day in the first quarter

item.title: Aramco profits up 25% despite Strait of Hormuz disruption
item.description: Company ramped up oil supply via East-West Pipeline to maximum capacity of 7 million barrels per day in the first quarter

item.title: Who won Aramco's lucrative long-term framework deals?
item.description: New long term agreement deals likely to further streamline Aramco's key onshore projects

item.title: SURF’s up for Indonesia gas project
item.description: Contractor lined up for key Mako subsea prize

item.title: Australia to reserve 20% of gas exports for domestic market
item.description: Federal government expects to finalise policy design at later stage after consultations

item.title: Australia gas project advances into early-stage FEED
item.description: Hancock Energy plans to build central processing facility to treat natural gas from nearby gas field

item.title: Petronas seeks contractor to shoot 3D seismic offshore Indonesia
item.description: Malaysian heavyweight keen to explore hydrocarbon potential of Serpang block awarded in 2025

item.title: Oil prices fall 6% over the week on hopes of long-awaited end to Iran war
item.description: Crude futures traded lower as Trump hints at a forthcoming conclusion to Strait of Hormuz crisis

item.title: DOF wins $2 billion newbuild vessels contract with Petrobras
item.description: RSVs will be built locally and are expected to start operations offshore Brazil in 2030

item.title: Trillions of cubic feet of gas locked in US Gulf Coast rock, says government review
item.description: Industry could still unlock over 343 Tcf of gas from formation tied to Haynesville shale, says USGS

item.title: Iraq Oil Ministry denies US-sanctioned official aided Iran
item.description: US authorities sanction Iraqi deputy oil minister for allegedly aiding scheme to disguise Iranian oil as Iraqi product to circumvent US prohibitions

item.title: Azerbaijani operator completes latest deep offshore well in Caspian Sea
item.description: International and local players drilling deeper to identify natural gas reserves as part of Baku's plans to increase gas exports to Europe

item.title: Arrow brings Colombia well on stream after pay hit
item.description: Well hits multiple hydrocarbon-bearing intervals

item.title: MOL profits slump as downstream woes outweigh oil price boost
item.description: Iran conflict and other disruptions affect oil and gas production but company optimistic of future output growth

item.title: Houston offshore gathering shrugs off Middle East volatility
item.description: Slow and steady wins the race for offshore operators attending OTC 2026 flagship event, many of whom are too focused on long-term plans to mind short-term price swings

item.title: Oil's new frontier? Sao Tome & Principe launches licensing round
item.description: African archipelago aims to build on industry interest in its deepwater plays

item.title: FPSO upgrade on cards at Eni asset in Ghana
item.description: Italian major signs deal to boost gas output from its Gye Nyame field

item.title: LNG sector seeing near-term ‘supply disruption’ not ‘demand destruction’, Excelerate says
item.description: Self-dubbed ‘boring’ infrastructure provider to swap around Pakistan FSRUs this year

item.title: Extension and clarity for FPSO on UK North Sea oilfield
item.description: FPSO on Catcher field gains several more years of work

item.title: FPSO wins extension for UK North Sea oilfield
item.description: FPSO on Catcher field gains several more years of work

item.title: DNO ‘sprinting’ to success in Norway with fast-track mindset
item.description: Company pushing joint venture partners to accelerate development of nine discoveries

item.title: Asian contracting heavyweight to chase $57.1 billion worth of energy projects
item.description: L&T is a leading contractor across key markets in India and the Middle East, with international projects contributing to more than 50% of its project portfolio
